title: [security] C schnorr48_verify: no identity/small-order/canonical pubkey validation - signature forgery under TOFU
description: lichen/subsys/lichen/link/schnorr48.c:135-194 schnorr48_verify() delegates all point validation to crypto_eddsa_recover_r (crypto/monocypher.c:2990-3005), which checks only on-curve (square-root check) and s < L. No identity, no small-order/torsion, no non-canonical encoding check. Python (python/src/lichen/crypto/schnorr48.py:129-130) uses crypto_core_ed25519_is_valid_point(pubkey); Rust (rust/lichen-link/src/schnorr.rs:117-120) requires !is_identity() && is_torsion_free(). Empirically verified: a forgery under the identity pubkey (pick s=r, e=H(r*B || A_identity || msg)[:16]; verification computes R'=s*B - e*A = r*B and the challenge matches) is ACCEPTED by C, rejected by Python and Rust. In LICHEN's TOFU trust model keys come from peers (announce/first-contact), so an attacker who enrolls an identity or 8-torsion 'pubkey' can thereafter forge frames as that peer. Compounding gap: the C test suite (lichen/tests/schnorr48/main.c:150-186) omits exactly the validation-strength vectors from test/vectors/schnorr48.json (identity pubkey, low-order 8-torsion pubkey, non-canonical s, zero s, truncated) - those vectors pass in C only via equation failure, masking the gap. Fix: reject identity/small-order/non-canonical pubkeys in schnorr48_verify (e.g. ge_has_small_order + identity check + canonical encoding check before recover_r), and add the omitted vectors to the C suite (cross-ref the vector-consumption bead).
